name: Supervision (Roboflow)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Roboflow project; no API costs

performance.notes: Visualization and processing overhead minimal; GPU required for inference models

agent_readiness.notes: Computer vision toolkit from Roboflow for post-processing and visualizing detection results. Model-agnostic: works with YOLO, Grounding DINO, SAM, RT-DETR. Detections class for unified results format. ByteTrack/SORT object tracking. Annotation drawing (boxes, masks, labels). Frame counting, line zones, polygon zones for counting objects crossing lines. Used by 80K+ developers.

gotchas: Requires separate detection model — supervision is post-processing only
ByteTrack may lose track IDs for fast-moving objects
Some utility functions have breaking API changes between versions
GPU needed for models — supervision itself CPU-only
best_when: Unifying outputs from multiple computer vision models into standard format
Annotating images/video with bounding boxes for visualization and datasets
avoid_when: When full inference stack needed (pair with YOLO/DINO for detection)
Simple single-model pipelines with no visualization needs
summary: Supervision is Roboflow's model-agnostic computer vision toolkit. Provides a unified Detections format, object tracking (ByteTrack), annotation drawing, and zone-based counting. Works with any detection model (YOLO, DINO, SAM). Used by 80K+ developers. Best for building complete CV pipelines with tracking, counting, and visualization on top of any detection backend.
